title: Status, Events, Logs, Metrics, Traces, Audit, and Layered Incident Response · Reconciliation Brief: K8s Status Conditions Events
summary: A release has green desired replicas but user failures, overwritten events, restarted containers, missing cluster logs, high-cardinality metrics, and no cross-layer correlation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s status conditions events increment in cluster and workload incident evidence record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Correlate spec, observedGeneration, conditions, reasons, messages, events, resource versions, controller ownership, and event retention without treating event order as durable history.
Combine namespaces, RBAC, quotas, network policy, storage classes, scheduling isolation, Pod security, admission, audit, and dedicated clusters according to trust.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Status Conditions Events · Theory K8s Status Conditions Events
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory workload restarts after revision 2; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example workload restarts after revision 2; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline. Competency probe: Correlate spec, observedGeneration, conditions, reasons, messages, events, resource versions, controller ownership, and event retention without treating event order as durable history. A defensible solution applies K8s Status Conditions Events, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cluster and workload incident evidence record.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-observability-debugging-incidents-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-observability-debugging-incidents-4
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked k8s observability debugging incidents 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      correlation: release-uid-request-trace
      status: generation-and-conditions
      events: timestamped-short-retention
      logs: current-previous-and-node
      metrics: resource-system-application
      audit: identity-verb-resource
      diagnosis: first-causal-layer
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pod,endpointslice -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous --timestamps
    failure: Reject green replica count as user proof, restart as diagnosis, events as durable history, kubectl logs as archival storage, or unbounded cardinality.
    repair: Preserve identity and time, correlate desired and observed layers, reproduce one fault cleanly, retain the regression, and document telemetry limits.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
